senators.name: Shelley Moore Capito
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from Duke University and M.Ed. from University of Virginia. Career as teacher, state legislator, and U.S. Representative before Senate. Duke provides strong academic foundation. Education background shows intellectual engagement. Long legislative tenure with deep expertise on infrastructure and environment. Solid but approach is more consensus-building than analytically driven.
experience: Senate Environment and Public Works Committee

senators.name: Jim Justice
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from Marshall University and M.B.A. from Marshall University. Career as coal and agriculture businessman — one of West Virginia's wealthiest individuals. Governor of West Virginia. Business executive background but governance approach is more instinct-driven than evidence-based or analytical.

WV-01.name: Carol Miller
WV-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from Columbia University. Career in family business (auto dealership) and state legislature. Columbia provides strong academic credentials but legislative approach is not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.

WV-02.name: Riley Moore
WV-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from West Virginia University and J.D. from WVU College of Law. Career as attorney and West Virginia State Treasurer. Legal and financial management background provides analytical skills. State Treasurer role required budget and investment analysis. However, legislative record too new to assess evidence-based governance.
education: J.D., West Virginia University College of Law
